title: Chole Bhature
description: Chole Bhature is one of North India's most iconic dishes, featuring spicy chickpea curry served with soft, fluffy deep-fried bhature. Popular in Punjabi cuisine, it is a hearty and satisfying meal enjoyed for breakfast, lunch, or dinner.

ingredients.title: For Chole (Chickpea Curry)
items: Chickpeas (Kabuli Chana) – 1½ cups (soaked overnight, about 8 hours)

ingredients.title: Ground Spices
items: Regular Red Chili Powder – ½ tsp

instruction.title: Step 1: Cooking the Chole
steps: Drain soaked chickpeas, add fresh water, tea bag, and salt. Pressure cook (or in Instant Pot) until soft but not mushy. Discard tea bag.
Heat oil/ghee in a deep pan. Add whole spices and let them crackle.
Add onions, sauté until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste, green chilies, sauté 1 min.
Add tomato puree, cook until oil separates.
Add ground spices (except garam masala & amchur). Cook 3–4 minutes.
Add boiled chickpeas with cooking water. Simmer 20–25 mins until thick and glossy.
Finish with butter, garam masala, amchur, and fresh coriander.

instruction.title: Step 2: Making the Bhature
steps: In a bowl, mix maida, sooji, salt, sugar, baking powder, and soda.
Add yogurt and oil, mix well. Add warm water gradually to make a soft dough.
Cover and rest for at least 2 hours.
Divide into balls, roll into oval/round discs.
Deep-fry in hot oil (190°C / 375°F) until puffed and golden.

instruction.title: Plating & Serving
steps: Serve hot chole in a deep bowl, topped with onion rings, green chili, lemon wedge, and coriander.
Serve bhature fresh and fluffy, alongside.
Optional: Accompaniments like pickled onions, mint chutney, or boondi raita.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Serve hot with sliced onions, lemon wedges, and green chilies.
Pair with pickled onions, mint chutney, or boondi raita.
Best enjoyed immediately while the bhature are hot and fluffy.

chefTips: For that signature restaurant taste, finish the chole with a spoonful of ghee and a squeeze of lime before serving.
